compiled_state.title: As a DSO plugin maintainer, I can record structured suggestions to a persistent cross-session store
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: A suggestion-record.sh script that writes immutable JSON files to .suggestions/ on the tickets orphan branch, with flock-based write serialization and graceful degradation
**Why**: This is the foundation for all suggestion capture paths — agent-driven, automatic, and sub-agent sentinel. Without persistent cross-session storage, friction observations are lost when sessions end
**Scope**:
- IN: suggestion-record.sh script (invokable via .claude/scripts/dso shim), .suggestions/ directory on tickets orphan branch, JSON schema with required fields (timestamp, session_id, source) and optional fields (observation, recommendation, metrics, affected_file, skill_name), flock-based write lock (.suggestions/.suggestion-write.lock), file naming convention (<timestamp>-<session-id>.json), ticket system isolation (ticket list and health scripts exclude .suggestions/)
- OUT: Stop hook integration (Story 3), PostToolUse hook integration (Story 4), retro consumption (Story 5), agent guidance (Story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, calling suggestion-record.sh with observation text creates a JSON file in .suggestions/ on the tickets orphan branch with timestamp, session_id, and source fields populated
  ← Satisfies: "Agents can record a structured suggestion to .suggestions/ on the tickets orphan branch via suggestion-record.sh"
- When this story is complete, the JSON format separates objective observations (what happened, optional metrics like wall-clock time and token counts) from subjective recommendations (what to change)
  ← Satisfies: "a format that separates objective observations from subjective recommendations"
- When this story is complete, concurrent writes from parallel sessions do not corrupt or lose suggestion files
  ← Satisfies: "Agents can record a structured suggestion" (reliability requirement from scenario analysis)
- When this story is complete, ticket list and ticket health scripts do not include suggestion records in their output
  ← Satisfies: "The ticket health script and ticket list do not include suggestion records"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Needs flock-based serialization — follow the pattern in ticket-flock-contract.md with a separate lock file (.suggestions/.suggestion-write.lock)
- [Reliability] Must gracefully handle absent tickets orphan branch (fresh installs, projects that skipped onboarding) — fail silently with a logged warning, never crash
- [Reliability] Must create .suggestions/ directory on first use (mkdir -p equivalent)
- [Performance] File naming uses <timestamp>-<session-id>.json — session ID must have sufficient entropy (full UUID or equivalent) to prevent collisions in parallel environments
- [Maintainability] Schema should be documented in a contract file for retro synthesis and future consumers

comments.body: Done definition update: Must include a contract document at plugins/dso/docs/contracts/suggestion-schema-contract.md defining field names, required vs. optional fields, file naming convention, and orphan-branch isolation invariant.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Done definition update: suggestion-record.sh must set gc.auto=0 in the suggestions worktree/orphan branch to prevent GC contention within the tool-call timeout ceiling, matching the ticket system's flock contract pattern.
comments.author: Claude

comments.body: Consideration: [Reliability] File naming must include a UUID component (not just timestamp + session-id) to prevent collisions when concurrent sub-agents in a sprint emit suggestions within the same millisecond.
comments.author: Claude
